data.title: Preformed Line Products (NASDAQ: PLPC) increases quarterly dividend by 5% from $0.20 to $0.21 per share.
data.description: In Cleveland on December 11, 2025, Preformed Line Products (NASDAQ: PLPC) had its Board of Directors approve a 5% surge in the company's quarterly cash dividend. This means the dividend will see a raise from $0.20 per share to $0.21 per share. This increase indicates a positive development for shareholders. The dividend is set to be paid out on...

data.title: Lifeway warns shareholders about nominees Smolyansky and Sent's questionable backgrounds, citing ethical failures.
data.description: The backgrounds of nominees selected by Edward Smolyansky and George Sent, Managing Director of Cascadia Capital, have raised serious concerns and suggest questionable judgment.

data.title: Broadcom's Q4 revenue reaches $18.015B, up 28% from previous year. GAAP net income $8.518B, Non-GAAP net income $9.714B. EBITDA $12.218B or 68% of revenue.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, there was a significant increase in revenue, reaching $18,015 million, which was 28 percent higher compared to the same period last year. The GAAP net income for the fourth quarter was reported at $8,518 million, while the Non-GAAP net income stood at $9,714 million. Additionally, the adjusted EBITDA for the same quarter amounted to $12,218 million, representing 68 percent of the total revenue.

data.title: Destination XL Group and FullBeauty Brands merge to become a leading omni-channel retailer in inclusive sizing, generating $1.2B in annual sales.
data.description: Forming one of the biggest omni-channel retail companies in the full inclusive sizing sector, this merger combines for around $1.2 billion in yearly net sales.

data.title: Serina Therapeutics appoints Dr. Joshua Thomas as VP, Head of Chemistry to support SER-252 for Parkinson's.
data.description: In Huntsville on December 11, 2025, Serina Therapeutics, Inc. (NYSE American: SER) shared news of the appointment of Joshua Thomas, Ph.D., as Vice President and Head of Chemistry. The company, a clinical-stage biotechnology firm, is advancing its lead Investigational New Drug (IND) candidate, SER-252, designed for advanced Parkinson's disease treatment utilizing its proprietary POZ Platform TM drug optimization technology. Dr. Thomas will manage both internal and external chemistry endeavors aimed at optimizing POZ-based candidates, facilitating a smooth transition from the discovery phase to development.

data.title: Navitas partners with Avnet to expand distribution network for high-power markets like AI, data centers, energy.
data.description: Navitas is broadening its global presence through a partnership with Avnet, expanding from its established Avnet Silica (Europe) collaboration. This venture aims to bolster support for burgeoning high-power sectors, including AI data centers, performance computing, energy and grid infrastructure, as well as industrial electrification. The collaboration is set to capitalize on rapid growth opportunities in these markets.

data.title: Stocks bounce back, S&P 500 hits record high despite Oracle's earnings worries.
data.description: The S&P 500 saw a slight increase as investors disregarded worries about Oracle's earnings report - a move that proved to be adequate.

data.title: Destination XL Group reported Q3 sales of $101.9 million & a net loss of $(0.08) per diluted share. They plan to merge with FullBeauty.
data.description: Destination XL Group, Inc. reported sales of $101.9 million, down 5.2% from the previous year, with a net loss of $(0.08) per diluted share for the third quarter of fiscal 2025. Adjusted EBITDA was $(2.0) million, compared to $1.0 million in the prior year quarter. Cash and investments were $27.0 million at the end of the quarter. The company's strategic initiatives focused on assortment, FiTMAP technology, and promotions to address evolving consumer dynamics. DXL also announced a merger with FullBeauty Brands. The company aims to increase private brand sales penetration and expand FiTMAP technology to drive customer engagement. Customers' price sensitivity impacted sales, and the company worked to manage inventory and margins in the volatile market. Marketing costs were up, and retail square footage expansion and digital commerce were vital areas of growth. The call for investors was scheduled to discuss the merger and financial results.

data.title: Mitek Systems, Inc. reports record fiscal 2025 revenue. Full-year organic growth, driven by SaaS, ended Sept 30, 2025.
data.description: In San Diego, Mitek Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: MITK) disclosed their financial results for the fourth quarter ending September 30, 2025, along with projections for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026. The company, known for digital identity verification and fraud prevention, experienced significant growth in fiscal 2025, with a notable uptick in SaaS growth contributing to overall organic growth. For the upcoming fiscal year 2026, Mitek has outlined guidance for further expansion and development in their services. More information can be found on their website at www.miteksystems.com.

data.title: Amcor will have a reverse stock split on January 15, 2026. Second quarter fiscal 2026 numbers will be split-adjusted.
data.description: Newly adjusted shares are anticipated to start trading on January 15, 2026. Second quarter fiscal 2026 per share data will be disclosed based on the adjusted split. The company in focus, Amcor plc, a major player in sustainable development, is listed as (NYSE: AMCR; ASX: AMC). Located in Zurich, the announcement was made on December 11, 2025.

data.title: lululemon athletica inc. reports Q3 fiscal 2025 results; $1.0 billion increase in stock repurchase program.
data.description: Vancouver-based company lululemon athletica inc. (NASDAQ: LULU) released their financial report for the third quarter of fiscal 2025, concluding on November 2, 2025. CEO Calvin McDonald mentioned a focus on enhancing the U.S. sector and sustaining growth in international markets. The company is seeing advancements in their strategies and anticipates positive outcomes from their implemented plans.

data.title: lululemon athletica CEO Calvin McDonald stepping down on Jan 31, 2026. He will serve as senior advisor until March 31, 2026.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, lululemon athletica inc. (NASDAQ: LULU) revealed that Calvin McDonald is set to resign from his role as Chief Executive Officer and Board member, effective by January 31, 2026. Mr. McDonald will support a seamless transition and continue as a senior advisor until March 31, 2026. The company's Board is collaborating on a thorough search for a new CEO in partnership with a prominent executive firm.

data.title: Helmerich & Payne CEO John Lindsay to retire on March 4, 2026; Trey Adams appointed as new CEO. Adams to stand for election to the Board.
data.description: In Tulsa, Oklahoma, the Board of Directors of Helmerich & Payne, Inc. (NYSE: HP) revealed that CEO John Lindsay will retire post the Annual Meeting of Stockholders on March 4, 2026. Following this, Raymond John “Trey” Adams, the current President, will take over as the new CEO. Adams is set to assume the position after the mentioned meeting and is also nominated to stand for election as a Board member during the Annual Meeting. Lindsay will continue to serve in a different capacity within the company.

data.title: Flowserve divests asbestos liabilities subsidiary BW/IP to Acorn Investment Partners, Oaktree Capital.
data.description: Flowserve Corporation (NYSE: FLS) disclosed that they have successfully finalized the sale of BW/IP - New Mexico, Inc., a subsidiary dealing with asbestos liabilities and insurance assets, to an affiliate of Acorn Investment Partners. Flowserve, a key provider in flow control products and services for global infrastructure markets, made the announcement today. Acorn, a portfolio company managed by Oaktree Capital Management, has acquired BW/IP from Flowserve.

data.title: Argan, Inc. declares $0.50 quarterly dividend per common share on Jan 30, 2026 for stockholders as of Jan 22, 2026.
data.description: A company based in Arlington, Virginia, Argan, Inc. (NYSE: AGX) has disclosed that their Board of Directors has approved a standard quarterly cash dividend of $0.50 per common stock share. The dividend is scheduled to be distributed on January 30, 2026, to shareholders registered as of January 22, 2026. David Watson, the President and CEO of Argan, emphasized the importance of providing value to shareholders through these regular dividends.

data.title: HIVE gauges interest in AI-Bitcoin infrastructure among Andean investors through Colombia listing, tapping into growing sector.
data.description: The listing of HIVE in Colombia offers Andean investors the opportunity to invest in the rapidly expanding digital infrastructure sector. As Bitcoin miners increasingly turn their attention towards artificial intelligence (AI) and high-performance computing (HPC), this move becomes significant. Colombia's market now has the chance to engage with this shift, tapping into the potential growth within the industry. By diversifying their investments and adapting to changes in the mining landscape, investors in the Andean region can access this evolving sector.

data.title: LightPath Technologies plans public offering of common stock, announced on Dec. 11, 2025 (NASDAQ: LPTH).
data.description: On December 11, 2025, in Orlando, Florida, LightPath Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: LPTH), a key player in advanced optics and imaging solutions for defense and commercial sectors, initiated a new project. The company specializes in next-gen technologies and serves a vital role in these industries.

data.title: Agree Realty (NYSE: ADC) declares monthly dividend of $0.262 per common share, an annualized increase of 3.6% to $3.144.
data.description: In Royal Oak, Michigan, Agree Realty Corporation (NYSE: ADC) reported the approval of a monthly cash dividend of $0.262 per common share by its Board of Directors. The declared dividend amounts to an annualized $3.144 per common share, showcasing a 3.6% rise from the previous annualized dividend of $3.036 per common share in the fourth quarter of 2024. Shareholders are set to receive the dividend on January 15, 2026.

data.title: Dyne Therapeutics closes public offering, selling 21.8M shares at $18.44 each for $402.5M gross proceeds.
data.description: Dyne Therapeutics, Inc., headquartered in WALTHAM, Mass., announced on December 11, 2025, that it successfully closed an increased underwritten public offering. The offering consisted of 21,827,549 shares of common stock sold at a public price of $18.44 per share, with an additional 2,847,071 shares issued due to the underwriters exercising their option to purchase more shares. The total gross proceeds for Dyne from this offering amounted to about $402.5 million, excluding underwriting discounts, commissions, and offering expenses. The entirety of the shares in the offering were provided by Dyne. Dyne Therapeutics is a clinical-stage company specifically dedicated to enhancing functionality for individuals afflicted with genetically induced neuromuscular disorders.

data.title: Crinetics begins pivotal adult trial of Atumelnant in Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia, building on Phase 2 success.
data.description: The Phase 3 clinical trial expands on promising Phase 2 findings by demonstrating fast and consistent decreases in critical disease indicators and clinical assessments for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
